summary: PhD candidate in Software Supply Chain Security (NC State, exp. 2026) with 3+ years of industry experience at Samsung Research. Published at ASE, TOSEM, and ACSAC. Expertise in empirical software engineering, dependency management, program analysis, and LLM-assisted repair. Chromium open-source contributor and competitive CTF problem setter.

skills.label: Frameworks
skills.value: React, Django, Tensorflow, Keras, PyTorch, LangChain, LLVM

skills.label: Research
skills.value: Statistical Methods, Program Analysis, Mining Software Repositories, Empirical Methods, Code Language Models

skills.label: Tools
skills.value: Git, Docker, PostgreSQL, Oracle/SQL, LaTeX, Android

experience.title: Graduate Research Assistant
bullets: Designed two novel metrics to characterize dependency-update practices of OSS packages, guiding dependency selection decisions for developers.
Identified which dependency version constraints (pinning vs. floating) minimize outdated and vulnerable dependencies — published at ASE 2025.
Measured security-sensitive API usage across open-source packages; compared functionally equivalent packages by security posture.
Devised a relative-positioning context-collection strategy using hybrid BM25 + FAISS retrieval, winning 3rd Place at JetBrains/Mistral AI's ASE 2025 Context Collection Competition.

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer
bullets: Led a team delivering Media Capture & Playback for Samsung Internet on Galaxy smartwatches using the Chromium engine.
Added W3C API support that unlocked 50% of Galaxy Store web apps to run on Samsung wearables.
Shipped two user-facing features — gesture-based volume control (swipe up/down) and predictive buffering for network-interrupted playback.
Proposed and prototyped a federated-learning recommendation engine to improve suggestions while preserving on-device user privacy.

experience.title: Open-Source Contributor
bullets: Contributed patches across Multimedia, Autofill, and other modules; provided code-review support to fellow contributors.

education.detail: Advisor: Dr. Laurie Williams  ·  Research Domain: Software Supply Chain Security
Key courses: Software Engineering, Software Security, Cryptography, System Attack & Defense, Advanced Network Security, LLM Security, Generative AI for SE

publications.title: Which Is Better For Reducing Outdated And Vulnerable Dependencies: Pinning Or Floating?
publications.desc: Empirical study on which version constraints minimize dependency staleness and CVE exposure.

publications.title: Research Directions In Software Supply Chain Security
publications.desc: Comprehensive survey and future research agenda for securing the software supply chain.

publications.title: If You Cannot Measure It, You Cannot Secure It
publications.desc: Proposed and evaluated seven metrics to quantify mitigation effectiveness of security controls.

publications.title: Towards A Taxonomy Of Challenges In Security Control Implementation
publications.desc: 73-challenge taxonomy across 8 categories, mapped to NIST security controls.

publications.title: Relative Positioning Based Code Chunking For Repository-Level Code Completion
publications.desc: Hybrid BM25+FAISS retrieval strategy; achieved 3rd place in JetBrains/Mistral AI competition.

publications.title: How Quickly Do Development Teams Update Their Vulnerable Dependencies?
publications.desc: Two metrics characterizing OSS teams' responsiveness to dependency vulnerability disclosures.

publications.title: What's In A Package? Visibility Into Dependencies Via Sensitive API Calls
publications.desc: Security-sensitive API analysis across OSS packages to surface hidden dependency risks.

publications.title: SecureImgStego: Keyed Shuffling-based Deep Learning For Secure Steganography
publications.desc: Deep-learning steganography model integrating keyed-shuffling encryption.

patents.title: Methods And Apparatus For Capturing Puddlegram In Smartphone Camera
patents.label: US Patent 2025
